what_it_does: Frames the integration design space for a noticeboard/vault as a grid of two independent axes — where the vault sits and how much Obsidian functionality is activated — and recommends a default while flagging the risk of round-trip writeback.
observations: Defines Axis A as vault placement: A1 point straight at channel-memory/noticeboard/ and A2 mirror into a separate vault folder.
Defines Axis B as graduated activation levels L0 (search + properties), L1 (board views), L2 (thin one-way enrichment with wikilinks), and L3 (round-trip writeback).
States that every A×B cell is a real configuration with a different cost.
Recommends L0–L2 with point-or-mirror as the honest default when the host is not master.
Warns that L3 is where the host risks quietly becoming the master.
Includes YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, source, state, title, channel, and a part_of link to board://item-4baaab55.
standouts: The warning that L3 round-trip writeback is the boundary where a host may become canonical master.
The insistence on two independent axes rather than a single linear choice.
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item format with YAML frontmatter and markdown body.
Uses a numbered P3 prefix in the title.
Uses bullet lists and bold labels for axes and levels.
Records a part_of link to a parent board item.
concerns: Does not define 'canonical store', 'host', or 'master' within the file.
Does not provide criteria for choosing A1 versus A2.
Describes L3 as 'the hard one' without enumerating specific failure modes.
notes: A design note that maps vault integration choices along location and depth-of-activation axes, and flags round-trip writeback as the risky master-switch boundary.
questions: What is the canonical store and how is it identified?
What does 'host not master' mean in this system?
What mechanisms keep L2 read-mostly and runtime-safe?
How would the A2 mirror be synchronized with the live store?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the file explicitly lays out the A×B option grid and gives a default recommendation consistent with its stated purpose.
apparent_themes: Separation of storage location from feature activation.
Graduated integration with increasing mutability risk.
Avoiding accidental promotion of a host to master.
intention_signals: It is trying to stop the team from treating Obsidian integration as a one-dimensional decision and from enabling bidirectional writeback before explicitly deciding the host should be authoritative.
novelty: The explicit L0–L3 ladder and the framing of L3 round-trip writeback as an authority-transition threshold rather than just a feature.
contribution: Provides a decision grid and risk framing for integrating noticeboard items with Obsidian, distinguishing vault placement from activation depth and marking round-trip writeback as the point where host authority may shift.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard design note that models Obsidian integration as a two-axis grid of vault location (point vs mirror) and activation level (L0 search/properties through L3 round-trip writeback), recommending L0–L2 for non-master hosts and warning that L3 risks making the host the master.
intention_for_embedding: To prevent collapsing vault integration into a single linear choice and to flag round-trip writeback as the boundary where a host may become the canonical master.
